citations.narrative:
The facility did not have medical records documenting the assessment and ongoing treatment of a two-year-old female
French Bulldog with a litter of nursing puppies. The dog, Cricket (M/C # 932002002690904), was observed to be very thin,
with the lumbar vertebrae and pelvic bones visible with minimal fat coverage. The licensee contacted the Attending
Veterinarian when the weight loss was first observed, and began feeding a high protein diet in addition to the dog’s
regular diet as directed, but neither the instructions given by the AV nor the increased feedings were documented.
Medical records are important to document any illness or injury that occurs and demonstrate that adequate veterinary
care is provided to ensure the health and well-being of the animal.
Dealers must keep medical records for dogs that includes the identity of the animal and make these records available for
APHIS inspection. If a problem is identified, (such as a disease, injury, or illness), the date, description of the problem,
examination findings, test results, plan for treatment and care, and treatment procedures performed, when appropriate.
To be corrected by May 8, 2026.
This inspection was conducted with facility representatives and the exit interview was conducted with the licensee.
n

narrative:
3.13(b)(2) 
   
Veterinary care for dogs. 
The facility did not have medical records documenting the assessment and ongoing treatment of a two-year-old female 
French Bulldog with a litter of nursing puppies. The dog, Cricket (M/C # 932002002690904), was observed to be very thin, 
with the lumbar vertebrae and pelvic bones visible with minimal fat coverage. The licensee contacted the Attending 
Veterinarian when the weight loss was first observed, and began feeding a high protein diet in addition to the dog’s 
regular diet as directed, but neither the instructions given by the AV nor the increased feedings were documented. 
 
Medical records are important to document any illness or injury that occurs and demonstrate that adequate veterinary 
care is provided to ensure the health and well-being of the animal. 
 
Dealers must keep medical records for dogs that includes the identity of the animal and make these records available for 
APHIS inspection. If a problem is identified, (such as a disease, injury, or illness), the date, description of the problem, 
examination findings, test results, plan for treatment and care, and treatment procedures performed, when appropriate.  
 
To be corrected by May 8, 2026. 
 
This inspection was conducted with facility representatives and the exit interview was conducted with the licensee.
